page: Microsoft Azure EC20ads_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECADCCV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 10 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It is equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is provided via a 768 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations show strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2701.27 placing it in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance is average, scoring 27002.45 on stress-ng. Memory performance is a key strength, with a PassMark memory score of 2826.59 in the top 25%, though uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain average at 76.83 GB/sec and 44.72 GB/sec respectively. For language model tasks, text generation using a small 135M model is strong at 299.69 tokens/sec, whereas prompt processing for large 70B models is poor at 4.13 tokens/sec.

The qualitative cost profile of this instance is defined by its high memory density and bundled local storage, which reduces the need for external storage attachments for temporary data. This makes it a resource-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated GPU acceleration. Appropriate workloads include relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, text and image processing, and software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure EC20ads_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 20 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 3.525 GHz, this x86_64 instance features a 768 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance and high memory efficiency, highlighted by a PassMark memory score in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance and uncached memory bandwidth fall within average ranges, and large-scale LLM processing is limited. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy operations, eliminating external storage overhead for temporary files. It is best suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC20ads_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 160 GB of RAM, and a 768 GB local disk for high-performance database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC20ads_cc_v5 is an AMD-powered virtual machine with 20 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 768 GB local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine with dedicated AMD EPYC processing, high memory density, and integrated local storage for demanding data workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 20-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
Includes 768 GB of local disk storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU and memory performance benchmarks
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and software compilation
